sentence: El cielo non es claro oy.
ipa: el tsjelo non es klaɾo oj
gloss_en: The sky is not clear today.
notes: Voiceless dental affricate ⟨c⟩ [ts] 'tsjelo' (pre-merger); Old Spanish negator 'non'; tonic Ĕ>ie diphthong (penny2002, lapesa1981).

sentence: Cinco cavallos e dos vacas.
ipa: tsinko kavaʎos e dos vakas
gloss_en: Five horses and two cows.
notes: ⟨c⟩ [ts] 'tsinko'; period spelling 'cavallos' with lateral ⟨ll⟩ [ʎ]; conjunction 'e' (penny2002, lapesa1981).

sentence: La mugier fabla con el fijo.
ipa: la muʒjeɾ fabla kon el fiʒo
gloss_en: The woman speaks with the son.
notes: Voiced palato-alveolar ⟨g⟩ [ʒ] 'muʒjeɾ'; F- retained 'fabla','fijo' (pre-aspiration change) (penny2002, lapesa1981).

sentence: Dixo el rey al conde de Castiella.
ipa: diʃo el rej al konde de kastjeʎa
gloss_en: The king spoke to the count of Castile.
notes: Voiceless palato-alveolar ⟨x⟩ [ʃ] 'diʃo'; initial trill ⟨r⟩ [r] 'rey'; diphthong 'Castiella' (penny2002, lapesa1981).

sentence: Plaze a Dios que venga el Cid.
ipa: pladze a djos ke venɡa el tsið
gloss_en: May it please God that the Cid come.
notes: Voiced dental affricate ⟨z⟩ [dz] 'pladze'; sandhi 'plaze + a'; tonic Ĕ>ie 'venga' base (penny2002, lapesa1981).

sentence: La casa es cosa buena e fermosa.
ipa: la kaza es koza bwena e feɾmoza
gloss_en: The house is a good and beautiful thing.
notes: Intervocalic ⟨s⟩ voices to [z] 'kaza','koza' vs voiceless elsewhere; F- retained 'fermosa'; tonic Ŏ>ue 'buena' (penny2002, lapesa1981).

sentence: Quántas gentes vienen a la corte?
ipa: kantas ʒentes vjenen a la koɾte
gloss_en: How many people are coming to the court?
notes: Voiced ⟨g⟩ [ʒ] 'ʒentes'; tonic Ĕ>ie 'vienen'; sandhi 'vienen + a' (penny2002, lapesa1981).

sentence: Assí lo dixo el buen conde.
ipa: asi lo diʃo el bwen konde
gloss_en: Thus the good count spoke.
notes: ⟨ss⟩ voiceless [s] 'asi' vs voiced intervocalic ⟨s⟩; ⟨x⟩ [ʃ] 'diʃo'; tonic Ŏ>ue 'buen' (penny2002, lapesa1981).

sentence: Si yo pudiesse, ternía aquella villa.
ipa: si jo puðjese teɾnia akeʎa viʎa
gloss_en: If I could, I would hold that town.
notes: ⟨ss⟩ [s] 'pudiese'; lateral ⟨ll⟩ [ʎ] 'aquella','villa'; ⟨y⟩ [j] 'yo'; conditional register (penny2002, lapesa1981).

sentence: Ayer merqué cinco panes e dos quesos.
ipa: ajeɾ meɾke tsinko panes e dos kezos
gloss_en: Yesterday I bought five loaves and two cheeses.
notes: ⟨c⟩ [ts] 'tsinko'; intervocalic ⟨s⟩ [z] 'kezos'; ⟨y⟩ [j] 'ayer' (penny2002, lapesa1981).

sentence: El año que viene iré a Sevilla.
ipa: el aɲo ke vjene iɾe a seviʎa
gloss_en: Next year I will go to Seville.
notes: Palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ] 'año'; lateral ⟨ll⟩ [ʎ] 'Sevilla'; tonic Ĕ>ie 'viene' (penny2002, lapesa1981).

sentence: Llama al escudero quando lleguares.
ipa: ʎama al eskuðeɾo kando ʎeɡaɾes
gloss_en: Call the squire when you arrive.
notes: Word-initial lateral ⟨ll⟩ [ʎ] 'llama','lleguares'; ⟨qu⟩ [k] 'quando'; imperative register (penny2002, lapesa1981).

sentence: Buenos días, cómo vos va la vida?
ipa: bwenos dias komo vos va la viða
gloss_en: Good day, how does life treat you?
notes: Tonic Ŏ>ue 'buenos'; intervocalic ⟨d⟩ [ð] 'vida'; ⟨v⟩ [v] distinct from ⟨b⟩ (pre-betacism) (penny2002, lapesa1981).

sentence: Más vale páxaro en mano que ciento bolando.
ipa: mas vale paʃaɾo en mano ke tsjento bolando
gloss_en: A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
notes: ⟨x⟩ [ʃ] 'paʃaɾo'; ⟨c⟩ [ts] with Ĕ>ie 'tsjento'; proverb register (penny2002, lapesa1981).

sentence: El perro corre por la ribera del río.
ipa: el pero kore poɾ la riβeɾa del rio
gloss_en: The dog runs along the riverbank.
notes: Trill ⟨rr⟩ [r] 'perro','corre' and initial ⟨r⟩ [r] 'río' vs tap ⟨r⟩ [ɾ] 'ribera'; intervocalic ⟨b⟩ [β] (penny2002, lapesa1981).

sentence: El niño pequeño sueña con la montaña.
ipa: el niɲo pekeɲo sweɲa kon la montaɲa
gloss_en: The small child dreams about the mountain.
notes: Palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ] 'niño','pequeño','montaña'; tonic Ŏ>ue 'sueña' (penny2002, lapesa1981).

sentence: La cibdad de Burgos es grande e rica.
ipa: la tsibdað de buɾɡos es ɡɾande e rika
gloss_en: The city of Burgos is large and rich.
notes: Voiceless dental affricate ⟨c⟩ [ts] 'tsibdad' in the period form 'cibdad'; conjunction 'e' (penny2002, lapesa1981).

sentence: Fijos e fijas del rey moro.
ipa: fiʒos e fiʒas del rej moɾo
gloss_en: Sons and daughters of the Moorish king.
notes: F- retained 'fijos','fijas' [f]; voiced ⟨j⟩ [ʒ] 'fiʒos'; initial trill ⟨r⟩ [r] 'rey' (penny2002, lapesa1981).

sentence: Non creo que llueva sobre aquellos cerros.
ipa: non kɾeo ke ʎweva sobɾe akeʎos tseros
gloss_en: I do not think it will rain over those hills.
notes: Old Spanish negator 'non'; lateral ⟨ll⟩ [ʎ] 'aquellos'; ⟨c⟩ [ts] and trill ⟨rr⟩ [r] 'cerros' (penny2002, lapesa1981).

sentence: El cavallero passa la puente con su espada.
ipa: el kavaʎeɾo pasa la pwente kon su espaða
gloss_en: The knight crosses the bridge with his sword.
notes: Lateral ⟨ll⟩ [ʎ] 'cavallero'; ⟨ss⟩ [s] 'passa'; tonic Ŏ>ue 'puente' (penny2002, lapesa1981).
